_**User (2025-02-09 13:47:51Z)**_

Why are the table columns offset in a strange way for the Ergebnisse für Spieltag table? There is a whitespace gap on column 2. Give me the possible reasons for it as a list

---

_**Agent (2025-02-09 13:47:51Z)**_

Let me analyze the code and provide possible reasons for the offset columns in the "Ergebnisse für Spieltag" table. Here's what I can identify:

1. **Inconsistent Column Definitions**: Looking at the code, the table is created using `createTableBootstrap3('tableLeagueWeek', data)`, but I don't see the actual column definitions in the provided code. The offset might be caused by mismatched column definitions with the actual data structure.

2. **Hidden Columns**: There might be a hidden column or a column with `display: none` that's creating the whitespace gap. This can happen if there's a column meant for internal use (like an ID column) that's not properly hidden.

3. **Bootstrap Grid System Conflict**: Since the table is using Bootstrap classes (evident from the class names and structure), there might be conflicting grid classes or responsive table classes causing the offset.

4. **CSS Padding/Margin Issues**: There could be unexpected padding or margin values being applied to specific table cells, especially if there are custom CSS rules targeting the second column.

5. **Table Header Groups**: The code shows usage of header groups in other tables (like in `tableConfig.headerGroups`). If the header groups are not properly aligned with the data columns, it could cause visual offsets.

6. **Data Structure Mismatch**: The data being fetched from `/league/get_league_week_table` might have a structure that doesn't match the table rendering logic, causing alignment issues.

To pinpoint the exact cause, I would need to:
1. See the implementation of `createTableBootstrap3()`
2. Check the actual data structure being returned from the API
3. Review any custom CSS rules applying to the table
4. Examine the browser's computed styles for the affected columns

Would you like me to investigate any of these specific areas further?

---

check for the reasons in createTableBootstrap3. List the ideas you have

        "contents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data) {\r\n    // Check if the data has columns and data\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Transform the data from array format to object format\r\n    const transformedData = data.data.map(row =\u003e {\r\n        const obj = {};\r\n        let index = 0; // Initialize index to track the position in the row array\r\n\r\n        // Loop through each column group\r\n        data.columns.forEach(colGroup =\u003e {\r\n            if (colGroup.columns) {\r\n                // Loop through each column in the group\r\n                colGroup.columns.forEach(col =\u003e {\r\n                    obj[col.field] = row[index]; // Assign the value from the row to the object\r\n                    index++; // Increment index for the next field\r\n                });\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        return obj; // Return the transformed object\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    // Process columns to ensure frozen properties are set and add group indices\r\n    const processedColumns = data.columns.map((colGroup, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n        const newColGroup = { ...colGroup };\r\n        ",

        "originalContents": "function createTableTabulator(tableId, data) {\r\n    // Check if the data has columns and data\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Transform the data from array format to object format\r\n    const transformedData = data.data.map(row =\u003e {\r\n        const obj = {};\r\n        let index = 0; // Initialize index to track the position in the row array\r\n\r\n        // Loop through each column group\r\n        data.columns.forEach(colGroup =\u003e {\r\n            if (colGroup.columns) {\r\n                // Loop through each column in the group\r\n                colGroup.columns.forEach(col =\u003e {\r\n                    obj[col.field] = row[index]; // Assign the value from the row to the object\r\n                    index++; // Increment index for the next field\r\n                });\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        return obj; // Return the transformed object\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    // Process columns to ensure frozen properties are set and add group indices\r\n    const processedColumns = data.columns.map((colGroup, groupIndex) =\u003e {\r\n        const newColGroup = { ...colGroup };\r\n        ",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 10,
            "line": 181
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 153
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/templates/components/tables.html"
      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "                ${createTableRows()}\r\n            \u003c/tbody\u003e\r\n        \u003c/table\u003e\r\n    &#96;;\r\n}\r\n\r\n\r\n// Function to convert hex color to RGB\r\nconst hexToRgb = (hex) =\u003e {\r\n    const bigint = parseInt(hex.slice(1), 16);\r\n    const r = (bigint \u003e\u003e 16) \u0026 255;\r\n    const g = (bigint \u003e\u003e 8) \u0026 255;\r\n    const b = bigint \u0026 255;\r\n    return [r, g, b];\r\n};\r\n\r\n// Function to get colors for groups\r\nconst getGroupColors = (headerGroups) =\u003e {\r\n    const endColor = '#2196F3';  // Bootstrap primary blue\r\n    const startRGB = [255, 255, 255];  // white\r\n    const endRGB = hexToRgb(endColor);\r\n    \r\n    return headerGroups.map((_, index) =\u003e {\r\n        if (index === 0) return 'rgb(255, 255, 255)';  // First group is white\r\n        if (index === headerGroups.length - 1) return endColor;  // Last group is primary blue\r\n        \r\n        const factor = index / (headerGroups.length - 1);\r\n        const r = Math.round(startRGB[0] + (endRGB[0] - startRGB[0]) * factor);\r\n        const g = Math.round(startRGB[1] + (endRGB[1] - startRGB[1]) * factor);\r\n        const b = Math.round(startRGB[2] + (endRGB[2] - startRGB[2]) * factor);\r\n        \r\n        return &#96;rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})&#96;;\r\n    });\r\n};\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "    document.getElementById(tableId).appendChild(tableWrapper); // Append new table\r\n\r\n    console.log(&#96;Table \"${tableId}\" successfully created/updated&#96;);\r\n}\r\n\r\n\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cstyle\u003e\r\n/* Custom Tabulator Styling */\r\n/* General Table Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator {\r\n    border: none; /* Remove default borders */\r\n    background-color: transparent; /* Make background transparent */\r\n    font-family: 'Your Custom Font', sans-serif; /* Use your site's font */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Header Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-header {\r\n    background-color: #f8f9fa; /* Light background for header */\r\n    color: #343a40; /* Dark text color */\r\n    font-weight: bold; /* Bold header text */\r\n    border-bottom: 2px solid #dee2e6; /* Subtle border */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Column Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-col {\r\n    border-right: 1px solid #dee2e6; /* Subtle column borders */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-col .tabulator-col-content {\r\n    padding: 10px; /* Add padding for better spacing */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Row Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-row {\r\n    border-bottom: 1px solid #dee2e6; /* Subtle row borders */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-row.tabulator-row-even {",

        "originalContents": "                // Loop through each column in the group\r\n                colGroup.columns.forEach(col =\u003e {\r\n                    obj[col.field] = row[index]; // Assign the value from the row to the object\r\n                    index++; // Increment index for the next field\r\n                });\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        return obj; // Return the transformed object\r\n    });\r\n\r\n    // Create table wrapper for horizontal scroll\r\n    const tableWrapper = document.createElement('div');\r\n    tableWrapper.className = 'table-responsive';\r\n\r\n    // Create table\r\n    const table = document.createElement('table');\r\n    table.className = 'table table-striped table-hover';\r\n\r\n    // Create header\r\n    const thead = document.createElement('thead');\r\n\r\n    // Add group headers\r\n    const groupHeaderRow = document.createElement('tr');\r\n    data.columns.forEach(colGroup =\u003e {\r\n        const th = document.createElement('th');\r\n        th.textContent = colGroup.title || '';\r\n        th.colSpan = colGroup.columns ? colGroup.columns.length : 1;\r\n        groupHeaderRow.appendChild(th);\r\n    });\r\n    thead.appendChild(groupHeaderRow);\r\n\r\n    // Add sub-headers",

        "contents": "        if (newColGroup.columns) {\r\n            newColGroup.columns = newColGroup.columns.map((subCol, subIndex) =\u003e {\r\n                const newSubCol = { ...subCol };\r\n\r\n                // Add subtle vertical lines between column groups\r\n                if (subIndex \u003c newColGroup.columns.length - 1) {\r\n                    newSubCol.cssClass = 'border-right'; // Add a class for right border\r\n                }\r\n\r\n                // Set the formatter for the first column to apply color\r\n                if (subIndex === 0 \u0026\u0026 groupIndex === 0) { // Assuming the first column is the position\r\n                    newSubCol.formatter = function(cell) {\r\n                        const team = cell.getRow().getData().team; // Assuming 'team' is the field for the second column\r\n                        const color = getTeamColor(team); // Get the color for the team\r\n                        const value = cell.getValue(); // Get the cell value\r\n\r\n                        // Create a colored circle with the content inside\r\n                        return &#96;\u003cdiv style=\"display: flex; align-items: center;\"\u003e",

_**Agent**_

Let me analyze the implementation of `createTableBootstrap3` and identify potential issues that could cause the column offset:

1. **Index Calculation Issue**: 
```javascript
const value = rowData[index + (groupIndex === 0 ? 0 : 2)];
```
There's a hardcoded offset of 2 in the index calculation for groups after the first one. This could cause misalignment if the data structure doesn't match this assumption.

2. **Fixed Width Column Positioning**:
```javascript
td.style.left = `${index * 100}px`;
```
The first two columns are given fixed positions with a hardcoded width of 100px. If the content or styling requires different widths, this could cause gaps.

3. **Sticky Column Implementation**:
```javascript
if (groupIndex === 0 && index < 2) {
    td.style.position = 'sticky';
    td.style.left = `${index * 100}px`;
    td.style.zIndex = '1';
    td.style.backgroundColor = '#fff';
}
```
The sticky positioning of the first two columns might be creating visual gaps due to the fixed positioning and background color.

4. **Column Group Structure**:
```javascript
data.columns.forEach((colGroup, groupIndex) => {
    if (colGroup.columns) {
        colGroup.columns.forEach((col, index) => {
```
The nested structure of column groups might not be properly accounting for all columns in the index calculation.

5. **Border Styling**:
```javascript
if (index < colGroup.columns.length - 1) {
    th.style.borderRight = '1px solid #dee2e6';
}
```
The border styling is only applied to non-last columns within a group, which could create visual inconsistencies.

To fix this, I would suggest:

1. Let's check the actual data structure being passed to the table to ensure the index calculation matches
2. Consider making the column widths dynamic based on content
3. Review the sticky column implementation and possibly adjust the positioning
4. Verify the column grouping structure matches the data
5. Add consistent border styling across all columns

Would you like me to investigate any of these specific areas further or help implement a fix?

        "contents": "@bp.route('/league/get_available_weeks')\r\ndef get_available_weeks():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": \"Season and league are required\"}), 400\r\n            \r\n        weeks = league_service.get_weeks(league_name=league, season=season)\r\n        return jsonify(weeks)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_teams')\r\ndef get_available_teams():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": \"Season and league are required\"}), 400\r\n            \r\n        teams = league_service.get_teams_in_league_season(league, season)\r\n\r\n        return jsonify(teams)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_history')\r\ndef get_league_history():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get query parameters\r\n        # print(\"GOTCHA!\")\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        #week = request.args.get('week')\r\n        \r\n        #print(f\"League History - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}\")\r\n        print(f\"League History - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        \r\n\r\n\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        #week = int(week) if week is not None else 0\r\n        \r\n        # Use LeagueService\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_history_table(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season,\r\n            week=None,\r\n            depth=None,\r\n            debug_output=False\r\n        )\r\n\r\n        \r\n        \r\n        print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        print(table_data)\r\n        print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        #transformed_data = DataDict().transform_dict(table_data)\r\n\r\n        # print(transformed_data.to_dict())\r\n        return jsonify(table_data)  # Make sure to jsonify the response\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "@bp.route('/league/get_available_weeks')\r\ndef get_available_weeks():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": \"Season and league are required\"}), 400\r\n            \r\n        weeks = league_service.get_weeks(league_name=league, season=season)\r\n        return jsonify(weeks)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_teams')\r\ndef get_available_teams():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": \"Season and league are required\"}), 400\r\n            \r\n        teams = league_service.get_teams_in_league_season(league, season)\r\n\r\n        return jsonify(teams)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_history')\r\ndef get_league_history():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get query parameters\r\n        # print(\"GOTCHA!\")\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        #week = request.args.get('week')\r\n        \r\n        #print(f\"League History - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}\")\r\n        print(f\"League History - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        \r\n\r\n\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        #week = int(week) if week is not None else 0\r\n        \r\n        # Use LeagueService\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_history_table(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season,\r\n            week=None,\r\n            depth=None,\r\n            debug_output=False\r\n        )\r\n\r\n        \r\n        \r\n        print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        print(table_data)\r\n        print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        #transformed_data = DataDict().transform_dict(table_data)\r\n\r\n        # print(transformed_data.to_dict())\r\n        return jsonify(table_data)  # Make sure to jsonify the response\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_league_week_table(self, league:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None) -\u003e Response:     \n        ...\n        data_collected['data'] = []\r\n\r\n        league_standing_data_groups = league_standings_data.groupby(Columns.team_name)\r\n        league_standing_data_groups = sorted(league_standing_data_groups, key=lambda x: x[1][Columns.points].iloc[0], reverse=True)\r\n\r\n        for idx, (team, group) in enumerate(league_standing_data_groups):\r\n            team_row = [idx+1, team]\r\n            for row in group.to_dict('records'):\r\n                rows_to_extract = [row[col] for col in columns_to_show_week]\r\n                team_row.extend(rows_to_extract)\r\n                rows_to_extract = [row[col] for col in columns_to_show_season]\r\n                team_row.extend(rows_to_extract)\r\n            data_collected['data'].append(team_row)\r\n\r\n        #print(data_collected)\r\n\r\n        return data_collected",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_details')\r\ndef get_team_week_details():\r\n    \r\n    season = request.args.get('season')\r\n    week = request.args.get('week')\r\n    team = request.args.get('team')\r\n    league = request.args.get('league')\r\n    \r\n    print(f\"Team Week Details - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}, team={team}\")\r\n\r\n    week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n    \r\n    details = league_service.get_team_week_details(league, season, team, week)\r\n    \r\n    return jsonify({'config': details})\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_seasons')\r\ndef get_available_seasons():\r\n    try:\r\n        seasons = league_service.get_seasons()\r\n        return jsonify(seasons)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_leagues')\r\ndef get_available_leagues():\r\n    try:\r\n        leagues = league_service.get_leagues()\r\n        return jsonify(leagues)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_honor_scores')\r\ndef get_honor_scores():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        \r\n        honor_scores = league_service.get_honor_scores(\r\n            league=league, \r\n            season=season, \r\n            week=week, \r\n            individual_scores=3, \r\n            team_scores=3, \r\n            indivdual_averages=3, \r\n            team_averages=3\r\n        )\r\n        return jsonify(honor_scores)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_honor_scores: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "@bp.route('/league/get_team_week_details')\r\ndef get_team_week_details():\r\n    \r\n    season = request.args.get('season')\r\n    week = request.args.get('week')\r\n    team = request.args.get('team')\r\n    league = request.args.get('league')\r\n    \r\n    print(f\"Team Week Details - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}, team={team}\")\r\n\r\n    week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n    \r\n    details = league_service.get_team_week_details(league, season, team, week)\r\n    \r\n    return jsonify({'config': details})\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_seasons')\r\ndef get_available_seasons():\r\n    try:\r\n        seasons = league_service.get_seasons()\r\n        return jsonify(seasons)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_available_leagues')\r\ndef get_available_leagues():\r\n    try:\r\n        leagues = league_service.get_leagues()\r\n        return jsonify(leagues)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_honor_scores')\r\ndef get_honor_scores():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        \r\n        honor_scores = league_service.get_honor_scores(\r\n            league=league, \r\n            season=season, \r\n            week=week, \r\n            individual_scores=3, \r\n            team_scores=3, \r\n            indivdual_averages=3, \r\n            team_averages=3\r\n        )\r\n        return jsonify(honor_scores)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_honor_scores: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",
        "range": {

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_league_history_table(self, league_name:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None, debug_output:bool=False) -\u003e Response:    \n        ...\n        # add dynamic columns for each week\r\n\r\n\r\n        for week in weeks:\r\n            data_table['columns'].append({'title': \"Week \" + str(week), \r\n                                          'columns': [\r\n                                            { 'title': \"Pins\", 'field': \"week\" + str(week) + \"_score\" },\r\n                                            { 'title': \"Pts.\", 'field': \"week\" + str(week) + \"_points\" }\r\n                                          ]})\r\n\r\n        # add static columns for seasons summary\r\n        data_table['columns'].append({'title': \"Season Total\",\r\n                                      'columns': [\r\n                                            { 'title': \"Pins\", 'field': \"season_total_score\" },\r\n                                            { 'title': \"Pts.\", 'field': \"season_total_points\" },\r\n                                            { 'title': \"Avg.\", 'field': \"season_total_average\" }\r\n                                          ]})\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n        # add data row by row\r\n        data_table['data'] = []\r\n\r\n        for idx, team in enumerate(team_order):\r\n            group = data_per_team.get_group(team)\r\n        #for idx, (team, group) in enumerate(data_per_team): \r\n            team_row = [idx+1, team]\r\n            for week in weeks:\r\n                team_row.append(int(group[group[Columns.week] == week][Columns.score].sum()))\r\n                team_row.append(int(group[group[Columns.week] == week][Columns.points].sum()))\r\n            \r\n            # add total points\r\n            team_row.append(int(group[Columns.score].sum()))\r\n            team_row.append(int(group[Columns.points].sum()))\r\n\r\n            team_row.append(round(float(group[ColumnsExtra.score_average].sum() / len(group)), 2))\r\n            data_table['data'].append(team_row)",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_league_week_table(self, league:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None) -\u003e Response:     \r\n        \"\"\"Get standings of a leagaue up to a specific match day\"\"\"\r\n        #print(\" \u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e doing history just for testing\")\r\n        #self.get_league_history_table(league, season, week, 3)\r\n        #print(\" \u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c done with history\")\r\n        league_standings_data = self.server.get_league_standings(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n        league_week_data = self.server.get_league_week(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        league_standings_data = league_standings_data.rename(columns={ColumnsExtra.score_average: ColumnsExtra.score_average_weekly, \r\n                                                          Columns.points: ColumnsExtra.points_weekly,\r\n                                                          Columns.score: ColumnsExtra.score_weekly})\r\n        league_standings_data = pd.merge(league_standings_data, league_week_data, on=Columns.team_name)\r\n        #print(\"league_standings_data\")\r\n        #print(league_standings_data)\r\n\r\n\r\n        league_standings_data = league_standings_data.sort_values(by=[Columns.points])\r\n\r\n        data_collected = dict()\r\n        columns_to_show_week = [Columns.score, Columns.points, ColumnsExtra.score_average]",

        "originalContents": "    def get_league_week_table(self, league:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None) -\u003e Response:     \r\n        \"\"\"Get standings of a leagaue up to a specific match day\"\"\"\r\n        #print(\" \u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e doing history just for testing\")\r\n        #self.get_league_history_table(league, season, week, 3)\r\n        #print(\" \u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c done with history\")\r\n        league_standings_data = self.server.get_league_standings(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n        league_week_data = self.server.get_league_week(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        league_standings_data = league_standings_data.rename(columns={ColumnsExtra.score_average: ColumnsExtra.score_average_weekly, \r\n                                                          Columns.points: ColumnsExtra.points_weekly,\r\n                                                          Columns.score: ColumnsExtra.score_weekly})\r\n        league_standings_data = pd.merge(league_standings_data, league_week_data, on=Columns.team_name)\r\n        #print(\"league_standings_data\")\r\n        #print(league_standings_data)\r\n\r\n\r\n        league_standings_data = league_standings_data.sort_values(by=[Columns.points])\r\n\r\n        data_collected = dict()\r\n        columns_to_show_week = [Columns.score, Columns.points, ColumnsExtra.score_average]",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_league_history_table(self, league_name:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None, debug_output:bool=False) -\u003e Response:    \r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"get_league_history_table\")\r\n            print(\"league_name: \" + league_name + \" season: \" + season + \" week: \" + str(week) + \" depth: \" + str(depth))\r\n        data = self.server.get_league_history(league_name=league_name, season=season, week=week, depth=-1, debug_output=debug_output)\r\n        # find all weeks\r\n        weeks = data[Columns.week].unique()\r\n        # groupby week\r\n\r\n        data_collected_by_week = dict()\r\n        data_per_week = data.groupby(Columns.week)\r\n        data_per_team = data.groupby(Columns.team_name)\r\n\r\n\r\n        team_order = self.get_team_positions_during_season(league_name, season)['order']\r\n\r\n        # collect by week\r\n        for week, group in data_per_week:\r\n            if debug_output \u0026 0 :\r\n                print(\"week : \" + str(week))\r\n                print(group)\r\n            data_collected_by_week[week] = group.to_dict('records')\r\n        # concat all weeks\r\n        # collect by team    \r\n        \r\n        data_table = dict()\r\n        ## Table Columns\r\n\r\n        # doing the static part \r\n        data_table['columns'] = [\r\n                     { 'title': 'Ranking', 'frozen': 'left', 'columns': [\r\n                        { 'title': \"#\", 'field': \"pos\" },\r\n                        { 'title': \"Team\", 'field': \"team\" } ]\r\n                        }]",

        "originalContents": "    def get_league_history_table(self, league_name:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None, debug_output:bool=False) -\u003e Response:    \r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"get_league_history_table\")\r\n            print(\"league_name: \" + league_name + \" season: \" + season + \" week: \" + str(week) + \" depth: \" + str(depth))\r\n        data = self.server.get_league_history(league_name=league_name, season=season, week=week, depth=-1, debug_output=debug_output)\r\n        # find all weeks\r\n        weeks = data[Columns.week].unique()\r\n        # groupby week\r\n\r\n        data_collected_by_week = dict()\r\n        data_per_week = data.groupby(Columns.week)\r\n        data_per_team = data.groupby(Columns.team_name)\r\n\r\n\r\n        team_order = self.get_team_positions_during_season(league_name, season)['order']\r\n\r\n        # collect by week\r\n        for week, group in data_per_week:\r\n            if debug_output \u0026 0 :\r\n                print(\"week : \" + str(week))\r\n                print(group)\r\n            data_collected_by_week[week] = group.to_dict('records')\r\n        # concat all weeks\r\n        # collect by team    \r\n        \r\n        data_table = dict()\r\n        ## Table Columns\r\n\r\n        # doing the static part \r\n        data_table['columns'] = [\r\n                     { 'title': 'Ranking', 'frozen': 'left', 'columns': [\r\n                        { 'title': \"#\", 'field': \"pos\" },\r\n                        { 'title': \"Team\", 'field': \"team\" } ]\r\n                        }]",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_league_week_table_deprecated(self, league:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None) -\u003e Response:     \r\n        \"\"\"Get standings of a leagaue up to a specific match day\"\"\"\r\n        #print(\" \u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e doing history just for testing\")\r\n        #self.get_league_history_table(league, season, week, 3)\r\n        #print(\" \u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c done with history\")\r\n        league_standings_data = self.server.get_league_week(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n        league_week_data = self.server.get_league_week(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        league_standings_data = league_standings_data.rename(columns={ColumnsExtra.score_average: ColumnsExtra.score_average_weekly, \r\n                                                          Columns.points: ColumnsExtra.points_weekly,\r\n                                                          Columns.score: ColumnsExtra.score_weekly})\r\n        league_standings_data = pd.merge(league_standings_data, league_week_data, on=Columns.team_name)\r\n        #print(league_standings_data)\r\n        return league_standings_data.to_dict('records') \r\n        # if no depth is provided, use 1 to fetch also the data of the current week\r\n        if depth is None or depth \u003c 1:\r\n            depth = 1\r\n\r\n        # history should not be deeper than the current week\r\n        depth = min(depth, week)",

        "originalContents": "    def get_league_week_table_deprecated(self, league:str, season:str, week:int=None, depth:int=None) -\u003e Response:     \r\n        \"\"\"Get standings of a leagaue up to a specific match day\"\"\"\r\n        #print(\" \u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e doing history just for testing\")\r\n        #self.get_league_history_table(league, season, week, 3)\r\n        #print(\" \u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c done with history\")\r\n        league_standings_data = self.server.get_league_week(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n        league_week_data = self.server.get_league_week(league_name=league, season=season, week=week)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        league_standings_data = league_standings_data.rename(columns={ColumnsExtra.score_average: ColumnsExtra.score_average_weekly, \r\n                                                          Columns.points: ColumnsExtra.points_weekly,\r\n                                                          Columns.score: ColumnsExtra.score_weekly})\r\n        league_standings_data = pd.merge(league_standings_data, league_week_data, on=Columns.team_name)\r\n        #print(league_standings_data)\r\n        return league_standings_data.to_dict('records') \r\n        # if no depth is provided, use 1 to fetch also the data of the current week\r\n        if depth is None or depth \u003c 1:\r\n            depth = 1\r\n\r\n        # history should not be deeper than the current week\r\n        depth = min(depth, week)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "    fetch(&#96;/league/get_team_week_details?season=${selectedSeason}\u0026league=${selectedLeague}\u0026team=${team}\u0026week=${currentWeek}&#96;)\r\n        .then(response =\u003e {\r\n            if (!response.ok) {\r\n                throw new Error(&#96;HTTP error! status: ${response.status}&#96;);\r\n            }\r\n            return response.json();\r\n        })\r\n        .then(response =\u003e {\r\n            //console.log(\"Received data:\", response);\r\n            \r\n            if (!response.config) {\r\n                throw new Error('Invalid response structure: missing config');\r\n            }\r\n\r\n            // Transform the data into array format\r\n            const transformedData = response.config.data.map(row =\u003e {\r\n                // Create an array matching the column order\r\n                return response.config.columns.map(col =\u003e row[col.key]);\r\n            });\r\n\r\n            const tableConfig = {\r\n                id: 'teamTableWeekDetails',\r\n                headerGroups: response.config.headerGroups,\r\n                columns: response.config.columns,\r\n                data: transformedData,  // Use transformed data\r\n                rowNumbering: response.config.rowNumbering || false,",

        "originalContents": "    fetch(&#96;/league/get_team_week_details?season=${selectedSeason}\u0026league=${selectedLeague}\u0026team=${team}\u0026week=${currentWeek}&#96;)\r\n        .then(response =\u003e {\r\n            if (!response.ok) {\r\n                throw new Error(&#96;HTTP error! status: ${response.status}&#96;);\r\n            }\r\n            return response.json();\r\n        })\r\n        .then(response =\u003e {\r\n            //console.log(\"Received data:\", response);\r\n            \r\n            if (!response.config) {\r\n                throw new Error('Invalid response structure: missing config');\r\n            }\r\n\r\n            // Transform the data into array format\r\n            const transformedData = response.config.data.map(row =\u003e {\r\n                // Create an array matching the column order\r\n                return response.config.columns.map(col =\u003e row[col.key]);\r\n            });\r\n\r\n            const tableConfig = {\r\n                id: 'teamTableWeekDetails',\r\n                headerGroups: response.config.headerGroups,\r\n                columns: response.config.columns,\r\n                data: transformedData,  // Use transformed data\r\n                rowNumbering: response.config.rowNumbering || false,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def deprecated_get_table(self, season, league, week=None, cumulative=False, week_=None):\r\n        \"\"\"Get combined table data for frontend\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        \r\n        \r\n        filters = {\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.league_name: league #,\r\n            #Columns.input_data: True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        try:\r\n            week_value = int(week) if week and week.strip() else None\r\n            print(f\"Processing week: {week_value}\")\r\n        except ValueError:\r\n            print(f\"Invalid match day value: {week}\")\r\n            week_value = None\r\n        \r\n        # Get match day standings\r\n        week_standings = pd.DataFrame()\r\n        if week_value is not None:\r\n            week_standings = self.get_league_stanings(\r\n                self.df, filters, week_value, cumulative=False, include_changes=False\r\n            )\r\n            print(f\"Match day standings found: {not week_standings.empty}\")\r\n        \r\n        # Get cumulative standings\r\n        season_standings = self.get_league_stanings(\r\n            self.df, filters, week_value, cumulative=True, include_changes=(week_value \u003e 1)\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        # Combine the data for frontend\r\n        combined_data = []\r\n        for _, row in season_standings.iterrows():\r\n            team_data = {\r\n                'Team': row['Team'],\r\n                'TotalPoints': row['Points'],\r\n                'Average': row['Average'] ,\r\n                'positionChange': row.get('PositionChange', 0)\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            if not week_standings.empty:\r\n                match_team = week_standings[week_standings['Team'] == row['Team']]\r\n                if not match_team.empty:\r\n                    team_data.update({\r\n                        'MatchPoints': match_team['Points'].iloc[0],\r\n                        'MatchAverage': match_team['Average'].iloc[0] \r\n                    })\r\n            \r\n            combined_data.append(team_data)",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "class LeagueService:\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    # fetches dataframes from server\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    # converts dataframes to dicts\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 15,

            "lineNumber": 407,

          {
            "lineNumber": 408,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 409,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 410,

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 416,
            "text": "        try:\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 419,
            "text": "        except ValueError:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 420,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 421,
            "text": "            week_value = None\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 422,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 423,
            "text": "        # Get match day standings\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 424,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 428,

          },
          {

          },

            "lineNumber": 430,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 431,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 435,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 436,
            "text": "        # Combine the data for frontend\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 437,
            "text": "        combined_data = []\r"

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 446,
            "text": "            if not week_standings.empty:\r"

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 449,

            "lineNumber": 450,

            "lineNumber": 451,

        "originalContents": "    def deprecated_get_table(self, season, league, week=None, cumulative=False, week_=None):\r\n        \"\"\"Get combined table data for frontend\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        \r\n        \r\n        filters = {\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.league_name: league #,\r\n            #Columns.input_data: True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        try:\r\n            week_value = int(week) if week and week.strip() else None\r\n            print(f\"Processing week: {week_value}\")\r\n        except ValueError:\r\n            print(f\"Invalid match day value: {week}\")\r\n            week_value = None\r\n        \r\n        # Get match day standings\r\n        week_standings = pd.DataFrame()\r\n        if week_value is not None:\r\n            week_standings = self.get_league_stanings(\r\n                self.df, filters, week_value, cumulative=False, include_changes=False\r\n            )\r\n            print(f\"Match day standings found: {not week_standings.empty}\")\r\n        \r\n        # Get cumulative standings\r\n        season_standings = self.get_league_stanings(\r\n            self.df, filters, week_value, cumulative=True, include_changes=(week_value \u003e 1)\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        # Combine the data for frontend\r\n        combined_data = []\r\n        for _, row in season_standings.iterrows():\r\n            team_data = {\r\n                'Team': row['Team'],\r\n                'TotalPoints': row['Points'],\r\n                'Average': row['Average'] ,\r\n                'positionChange': row.get('PositionChange', 0)\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            if not week_standings.empty:\r\n                match_team = week_standings[week_standings['Team'] == row['Team']]\r\n                if not match_team.empty:\r\n                    team_data.update({\r\n                        'MatchPoints': match_team['Points'].iloc[0],\r\n                        'MatchAverage': match_team['Average'].iloc[0] \r\n                    })\r\n            \r\n            combined_data.append(team_data)",

        "contents": "        .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            createTableTabulator('leagueTableHistory', data);\r\n            //createTableBootstrap2('leagueTableHistory', data);\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(error =\u003e console.error('Error:', error));\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction updateTableLeagueWeek() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const currentWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n\r\n    \r\n    console.log('Attempting to update TableLeagueWeek with:', {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: currentWeek\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !currentWeek) {\r\n        console.log(\"Missing required selection\");\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_league_week_table?season=${selectedSeason}\u0026league=${selectedLeague}\u0026week=${currentWeek}\u0026depth=${1}&#96;)\r\n        .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            createTableBootstrap3('tableLeagueWeek', data);",

        "originalContents": "        .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            createTableTabulator('leagueTableHistory', data);\r\n            //createTableBootstrap2('leagueTableHistory', data);\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(error =\u003e console.error('Error:', error));\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction updateTableLeagueWeek() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const currentWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n\r\n    \r\n    console.log('Attempting to update TableLeagueWeek with:', {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: currentWeek\r\n    });\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !currentWeek) {\r\n        console.log(\"Missing required selection\");\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_league_week_table?season=${selectedSeason}\u0026league=${selectedLeague}\u0026week=${currentWeek}\u0026depth=${1}&#96;)\r\n        .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            createTableBootstrap3('tableLeagueWeek', data);",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league:str, season:str, team:str, week:int) -\u003e Response:\r\n        data = self.server.get_team_week_details(league_name=league, season=season, team_name=team, week=week)\r\n        \r\n        print(data)\r\n        \r\n        return data\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n    def sort_by_cumulative_points_in_last_available_week(self, df):\r\n        # Get the last week's data and sort by PointsCumulative\r\n        last_week = df[df[Columns.week] == df[Columns.week].max()]\r\n        final_order = last_week.sort_values(ColumnsExtra.points_cumulative, ascending=False)[Columns.team_name].tolist()\r\n        \r\n        # Create a categorical type with the desired order\r\n        df[Columns.team_name] = pd.Categorical(df[Columns.team_name], categories=final_order, ordered=True)\r\n        \r\n        # Sort by Week and then Team (which now follows the final standings order)\r\n        sorted_df = df.sort_values([Columns.week, Columns.team_name])\r\n        \r\n        return sorted_df\r\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_points_during_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team points for each week during the season.\"\"\"\r\n        rankings = self.server.get_team_positions_and_points_during_season(league_name, season)\r\n        #print(\"league_service.get_team_positions_during_season\")\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        rankings = self.sort_by_cumulative_points_in_last_available_week(rankings)\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        rankings_grouped = rankings.groupby(Columns.team_name, observed=True)\r\n        \r\n        rankings_dict = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['weekly'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['cumulative'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['order'] = []\r\n\r\n\r\n        for team, group in rankings_grouped:\r\n            rankings_dict['weekly'][team] = group[Columns.points].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['cumulative'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.points_cumulative].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['order'].append(team)\r\n\r\n        return rankings_dict",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "    def get_team_week_details(self, league:str, season:str, team:str, week:int) -\u003e Response:\r\n        data = self.server.get_team_week_details(league_name=league, season=season, team_name=team, week=week)\r\n        \r\n        print(data)\r\n        \r\n        return data\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n    def sort_by_cumulative_points_in_last_available_week(self, df):\r\n        # Get the last week's data and sort by PointsCumulative\r\n        last_week = df[df[Columns.week] == df[Columns.week].max()]\r\n        final_order = last_week.sort_values(ColumnsExtra.points_cumulative, ascending=False)[Columns.team_name].tolist()\r\n        \r\n        # Create a categorical type with the desired order\r\n        df[Columns.team_name] = pd.Categorical(df[Columns.team_name], categories=final_order, ordered=True)\r\n        \r\n        # Sort by Week and then Team (which now follows the final standings order)\r\n        sorted_df = df.sort_values([Columns.week, Columns.team_name])\r\n        \r\n        return sorted_df\r\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_points_during_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team points for each week during the season.\"\"\"\r\n        rankings = self.server.get_team_positions_and_points_during_season(league_name, season)\r\n        #print(\"league_service.get_team_positions_during_season\")\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        rankings = self.sort_by_cumulative_points_in_last_available_week(rankings)\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        rankings_grouped = rankings.groupby(Columns.team_name, observed=True)\r\n        \r\n        rankings_dict = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['weekly'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['cumulative'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['order'] = []\r\n\r\n\r\n        for team, group in rankings_grouped:\r\n            rankings_dict['weekly'][team] = group[Columns.points].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['cumulative'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.points_cumulative].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['order'].append(team)\r\n\r\n        return rankings_dict",
        "range": {

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\nleague_service = LeagueService()\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/stats')\r\ndef stats():\r\n    try:\r\n        weeks = league_service.get_weeks()\r\n        print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n        return render_template('league/stats.html', \r\n                            seasons=league_service.get_seasons(),\r\n                            leagues=league_service.get_leagues(),\r\n                            weeks=weeks)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in stats route: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_combinations')\r\ndef get_combinations():\r\n    try:\r\n        return jsonify(league_service.get_valid_combinations())\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_combinations: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\nleague_service = LeagueService()\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/stats')\r\ndef stats():\r\n    try:\r\n        weeks = league_service.get_weeks()\r\n        print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n        return render_template('league/stats.html', \r\n                            seasons=league_service.get_seasons(),\r\n                            leagues=league_service.get_leagues(),\r\n                            weeks=weeks)\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in stats route: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_combinations')\r\ndef get_combinations():\r\n    try:\r\n        return jsonify(league_service.get_valid_combinations())\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_combinations: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def deprecated_get_table1(self, df: pd.DataFrame, filters: dict, week: int, cumulative: bool, include_changes: bool):\r\n        \r\n\r\n        df_filtered = query_database(df, filters)\r\n        # Apply match day filter\r\n        if week is not None:\r\n            if cumulative:\r\n                df_filtered = df_filtered[df_filtered[Columns.week] \u003c= week]\r\n            else:\r\n                df_filtered = df_filtered[df_filtered[Columns.week] == week]\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate standings\r\n        if not df_filtered.empty:\r\n            standings = df_filtered.groupby('Team').agg({\r\n                'Points': 'sum',\r\n                'Score': 'mean'\r\n            }).reset_index()\r\n            print(standings)\r\n            standings.columns = ['Team', 'Points', 'Average']\r\n            standings = standings.sort_values(['Points', 'Average'], ascending=[False, False])\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate position changes if requested\r\n            if include_changes and week and week \u003e 1:\r\n                # Get previous match day standings\r\n                prev_filters = filters.copy()\r\n                df_prev = query_database(df, prev_filters)\r\n                df_prev = df_prev[df_prev[Columns.week] \u003c= (week - 1)]\r\n                \r\n                prev_standings = df_prev.groupby('Team').agg({\r\n                    'Points': 'sum',\r\n                    'Score': 'mean'\r\n                }).reset_index()\r\n                \r\n                prev_standings.columns = ['Team', 'Points', 'Average']\r\n                prev_standings = prev_standings.sort_values(['Points', 'Average'], ascending=[False, False])\r\n                \r\n                # Calculate position changes (fixed calculation)\r\n                current_positions = {team: pos for pos, team in enumerate(standings['Team'], 1)}\r\n                prev_positions = {team: pos for pos, team in enumerate(prev_standings['Team'], 1)}\r\n                \r\n                # Positive number means team moved up, negative means team moved down\r\n                standings['PositionChange'] = standings['Team'].apply(\r\n                    lambda team: prev_positions.get(team, 0) - current_positions.get(team, 0)\r\n                )",

        "originalContents": "    def deprecated_get_table1(self, df: pd.DataFrame, filters: dict, week: int, cumulative: bool, include_changes: bool):\r\n        \r\n\r\n        df_filtered = query_database(df, filters)\r\n        # Apply match day filter\r\n        if week is not None:\r\n            if cumulative:\r\n                df_filtered = df_filtered[df_filtered[Columns.week] \u003c= week]\r\n            else:\r\n                df_filtered = df_filtered[df_filtered[Columns.week] == week]\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate standings\r\n        if not df_filtered.empty:\r\n            standings = df_filtered.groupby('Team').agg({\r\n                'Points': 'sum',\r\n                'Score': 'mean'\r\n            }).reset_index()\r\n            print(standings)\r\n            standings.columns = ['Team', 'Points', 'Average']\r\n            standings = standings.sort_values(['Points', 'Average'], ascending=[False, False])\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate position changes if requested\r\n            if include_changes and week and week \u003e 1:\r\n                # Get previous match day standings\r\n                prev_filters = filters.copy()\r\n                df_prev = query_database(df, prev_filters)\r\n                df_prev = df_prev[df_prev[Columns.week] \u003c= (week - 1)]\r\n                \r\n                prev_standings = df_prev.groupby('Team').agg({\r\n                    'Points': 'sum',\r\n                    'Score': 'mean'\r\n                }).reset_index()\r\n                \r\n                prev_standings.columns = ['Team', 'Points', 'Average']\r\n                prev_standings = prev_standings.sort_values(['Points', 'Average'], ascending=[False, False])\r\n                \r\n                # Calculate position changes (fixed calculation)\r\n                current_positions = {team: pos for pos, team in enumerate(standings['Team'], 1)}\r\n                prev_positions = {team: pos for pos, team in enumerate(prev_standings['Team'], 1)}\r\n                \r\n                # Positive number means team moved up, negative means team moved down\r\n                standings['PositionChange'] = standings['Team'].apply(\r\n                    lambda team: prev_positions.get(team, 0) - current_positions.get(team, 0)\r\n                )",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "    data.weeks.forEach(() =\u003e {\r\n        subheaderRow.innerHTML += &#96;\r\n            \u003cth\u003ePoints\u003c/th\u003e\r\n            \u003cth\u003eAverage\u003c/th\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    });\r\n    table.querySelector('thead').appendChild(subheaderRow);\r\n    \r\n    // Update body\r\n    const tbody = table.querySelector('tbody');\r\n    tbody.innerHTML = '';\r\n    \r\n    data.data.forEach(teamData =\u003e {\r\n        const row = document.createElement('tr');\r\n        row.innerHTML = &#96;\u003ctd\u003e${teamData.team}\u003c/td\u003e&#96;;\r\n        \r\n        teamData.weeks.forEach(week =\u003e {\r\n            row.innerHTML += &#96;\r\n                \u003ctd\u003e${week.points.toFixed(1)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003ctd\u003e${week.average.toFixed(2)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n            &#96;;\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        tbody.appendChild(row);\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction updateSectionLeagueStandings() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague) {\r\n        console.log(\"Missing required selection\");\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_league_history?season=${selectedSeason}\u0026league=${selectedLeague}&#96;)",

        "originalContents": "    data.weeks.forEach(() =\u003e {\r\n        subheaderRow.innerHTML += &#96;\r\n            \u003cth\u003ePoints\u003c/th\u003e\r\n            \u003cth\u003eAverage\u003c/th\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    });\r\n    table.querySelector('thead').appendChild(subheaderRow);\r\n    \r\n    // Update body\r\n    const tbody = table.querySelector('tbody');\r\n    tbody.innerHTML = '';\r\n    \r\n    data.data.forEach(teamData =\u003e {\r\n        const row = document.createElement('tr');\r\n        row.innerHTML = &#96;\u003ctd\u003e${teamData.team}\u003c/td\u003e&#96;;\r\n        \r\n        teamData.weeks.forEach(week =\u003e {\r\n            row.innerHTML += &#96;\r\n                \u003ctd\u003e${week.points.toFixed(1)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003ctd\u003e${week.average.toFixed(2)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n            &#96;;\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        tbody.appendChild(row);\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction updateSectionLeagueStandings() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague) {\r\n        console.log(\"Missing required selection\");\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_league_history?season=${selectedSeason}\u0026league=${selectedLeague}&#96;)",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "@bp.route('/league/get_team_points')\r\ndef get_team_points():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n           \r\n\r\n        print(f\"Team Points - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        points = league_service.get_team_points_during_season(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        #print(points)\r\n        return jsonify(points)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_points: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_points_vs_average')\r\ndef get_team_points_vs_average():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n           \r\n\r\n        print(f\"Team Points vs Average - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        points_raw = league_service.get_team_points_during_season(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )[\"weekly\"]\r\n\r\n        averages_raw = league_service.get_team_averages_during_season(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )[\"averages\"]\r\n       \r\n        #print(points_raw)\r\n        #print(averages_raw)\r\n\r\n        points_vs_average = dict()\r\n\r\n        for team, points in points_raw.items():\r\n            averages = averages_raw[team]\r\n            points_vs_average[team] = [[averages[i] , points[i]] for i in range(len(points))]\r\n       \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        #print(points_vs_average)    \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        return jsonify(points_vs_average)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_positions: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "@bp.route('/league/get_team_points')\r\ndef get_team_points():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n           \r\n\r\n        print(f\"Team Points - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        points = league_service.get_team_points_during_season(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        #print(points)\r\n        return jsonify(points)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_points: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_team_points_vs_average')\r\ndef get_team_points_vs_average():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n           \r\n\r\n        print(f\"Team Points vs Average - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        points_raw = league_service.get_team_points_during_season(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )[\"weekly\"]\r\n\r\n        averages_raw = league_service.get_team_averages_during_season(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )[\"averages\"]\r\n       \r\n        #print(points_raw)\r\n        #print(averages_raw)\r\n\r\n        points_vs_average = dict()\r\n\r\n        for team, points in points_raw.items():\r\n            averages = averages_raw[team]\r\n            points_vs_average[team] = [[averages[i] , points[i]] for i in range(len(points))]\r\n       \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        #print(points_vs_average)    \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        return jsonify(points_vs_average)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_team_positions: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_team_positions_during_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team positions for each week during the season.\"\"\"\r\n        rankings = self.server.get_team_positions_and_points_during_season(league_name, season)\r\n        #print(\"league_service.get_team_positions_during_season\")\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        rankings = self.sort_by_cumulative_points_in_last_available_week(rankings)\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        rankings_grouped = rankings.groupby(Columns.team_name, observed=True)\r\n        \r\n        rankings_dict = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['weekly'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['cumulative'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['order'] = []\r\n\r\n        for team, group in rankings_grouped:\r\n            rankings_dict['weekly'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.position_weekly].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['cumulative'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.position_cumulative].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['order'].append(team)\r\n\r\n        return rankings_dict\r\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_averages_during_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team average points for each week during the season.\"\"\"\r\n        teams_and_averages = self.server.get_team_averages_during_season(league_name, season).groupby(Columns.team_name)\r\n        \r\n        averages = dict()\r\n        averages['averages'] = dict()\r\n        \r\n        for team, group in teams_and_averages:\r\n            averages['averages'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.score_average].tolist()\r\n\r\n        return averages",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "isSignature": true,

        "originalContents": "    def get_team_positions_during_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team positions for each week during the season.\"\"\"\r\n        rankings = self.server.get_team_positions_and_points_during_season(league_name, season)\r\n        #print(\"league_service.get_team_positions_during_season\")\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        rankings = self.sort_by_cumulative_points_in_last_available_week(rankings)\r\n        #print(rankings)\r\n\r\n        \r\n        rankings_grouped = rankings.groupby(Columns.team_name, observed=True)\r\n        \r\n        rankings_dict = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['weekly'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['cumulative'] = dict()\r\n        rankings_dict['order'] = []\r\n\r\n        for team, group in rankings_grouped:\r\n            rankings_dict['weekly'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.position_weekly].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['cumulative'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.position_cumulative].tolist()\r\n            rankings_dict['order'].append(team)\r\n\r\n        return rankings_dict\r\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_averages_during_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get team average points for each week during the season.\"\"\"\r\n        teams_and_averages = self.server.get_team_averages_during_season(league_name, season).groupby(Columns.team_name)\r\n        \r\n        averages = dict()\r\n        averages['averages'] = dict()\r\n        \r\n        for team, group in teams_and_averages:\r\n            averages['averages'][team] = group[ColumnsExtra.score_average].tolist()\r\n\r\n        return averages",

_**Agent**_

Let me analyze the data structure and the table creation code. I can see that in `get_league_week_table()` method, the data structure being returned looks like this:

```python
data_collected = {
    'columns': [
        { 
            'title': 'Ranking', 
            'frozen': 'left', 
            'columns': [
                { 'title': "#", 'field': "pos" },
                { 'title': "Team", 'field': "team" }
            ]
        },
        {
            'title': "Spieltag " + str(week),
            'columns': [
                { 'title': "Pins", 'field': "week" + str(week) + "_score" },
                { 'title': "Pts.", 'field': "week" + str(week) + "_points" },
                { 'title': "Avg.", 'field': "week" + str(week) + "_average" }
            ]
        },
        {
            'title': "Season Total",
            'columns': [
                { 'title': "Pins", 'field': "season_total_score" },
                { 'title': "Pts.", 'field': "season_total_points" },
                { 'title': "Avg.", 'field': "season_total_average" }
            ]
        }
    ],
    'data': [...]  # Array of arrays with the actual data
}
```

The issue appears to be in the index calculation in `createTableBootstrap3`. Here's the problematic part:

```javascript
const value = rowData[index + (groupIndex === 0 ? 0 : 2)];
```

This is causing the offset because:

1. For the first group (Ranking), it uses indices 0 and 1
2. For subsequent groups, it adds 2 to the index, but this doesn't properly account for the number of columns in previous groups

Let me propose a fix. I'll edit the `createTableBootstrap3` function to properly handle the column indices:

_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

   ctx.save();\r\n                                            ctx.textAlign = 'center';\r\n                                            ctx.textBaseline = 'middle';\r\n                                            ctx.font = 'bold 12px Arial';\r\n                                            ctx.fillStyle = getTeamColor(teamName);\r\n                                            ctx.fillText(position.toString(), point.x, point.y);\r\n                                            ctx.restore();\r\n                                        });\r\n                                    }\r\n                                }\r\n                            }\r\n                        },\r\n                        plugins: [{\r\n                            id: 'customLabels',\r\n                            afterDatasetsDraw(chart) {\r\n                                const {ctx} = chart;\r\n                                const meta = chart.getDatasetMeta(0);\r\n                                \r\n                                meta.data.forEach((point, index) =\u003e {\r\n                                    const season = seasons[index];\r\n                                    const position = data[season].final_position;\r\n                                    \r\n                                    ctx.save();\r\n                                    ctx.textAlign = 'center';\r\n                                    ctx.textBaseline = 'middle';\r\n                                    ctx.font = 'bold 12px Arial';\r\n                                    ctx.fillStyle = getTeamColor(teamName);\r\n                                    ctx.fillText(position.toString(), point.x, point.y);\r\n                                    ctx.restore();\r\n                                });\r\n                            }\r\n                        }]\r\n                    });\r\n\r\n                    // Transform data for table\r\n                    const tableConfig = {\r\n                        data: seasons.map(season =\u003e [\r\n                            season,\r\n                            data[season].league_name,\r\n                            data[season].final_position\r\n                        ]),\r\n                        columns: [\r\n                            { title: 'Saison' },\r\n                            { title: 'Liga' },\r\n                            { title: 'Platzierung' }\r\n                        ],\r\n                        headerGroups: []\r\n                    };\r\n\r\n                    document.getElementById('tableTeamHistory').innerHTML = createTable(tableConfig);\r\n                });\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        function updateTeamAverages(teamName) {\r\n            fetch(&#96;/team/get_average_per_season?team_name=${teamName}&#96;)\r\n                .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n                .then(data =\u003e {\r\n                    const seasons = Object.keys(data);\r\n                    const averages = seasons.map(season =\u003e data[season]);\r\n                    \r\n                    createLineChart_vanilla(\r\n                        {[teamName]: averages},\r\n                        'chartTeamPerformance',\r\n                        'Durchschnittliche Leistung',\r\n                        seasons,\r\n                        false  // Don't reverse Y-axis for averages\r\n                    );\r\n                });\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        function loadSpecialMatches(teamName) {\r\n            fetch(&#96;/team/get_special_matches?team_name=${encodeURIComponent(teamName)}&#96;)\r\n                .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n                .then(data =\u003e displaySpecialMatches(data));\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        function displaySpecialMatches(data) {\r\n            // Helper function to format event string\r\n            const formatEvent = (match) =\u003e &#96;${match.Season} ${match.League} W${match.Week}&#96;;\r\n\r\n            // Populate highest scores\r\n            document.getElementById('highestScoresBody').innerHTML = data.highest_scores.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate lowest scores\r\n            document.getElementById('lowestScoresBody').innerHTML = data.lowest_scores.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate biggest wins\r\n            document.getElementById('biggestWinsBody').innerHTML = data.biggest_win_margin.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e+${match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score} : ${match.Score - match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate biggest losses\r\n            document.getElementById('biggestLossesBody').innerHTML = data.biggest_loss_margin.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score} : ${match.Score - match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n        }\r\n    \u003c/script\u003e\r\n{% endblock %} ",
      "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/templates/team/stats.html"
    },
    {
      "contents": "{% extends \"base.html\" %}\r\n\r\n{% block title %}Home{% endblock %}\r\n\r\n{% block content %}\r\n\u003c!--h1\u003eBowling Dashboard\u003c/h1--\u003e\r\n\u003cdiv class=\"row mt-4\"\u003e\r\n    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"card\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r\n                Info\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r\n                \u003c!-- We'll add stats here later --\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eDiese Seite ist ein Proof of Concept zur Darstellung von Ergebnissen und Statistiken rund um Bowling Ligen, Tuniere und Pokalwettbewerbe.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eDie Auswertungen sind oben über die Navigation zu \"League Stats\" und \"Player Stats\" zu finden.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eHauptaugenmerk liegt derzeit auf den Ligastatistiken. Alle Daten sind fiktiv, jedoch ist die Seite so aufgabeut, dass reale Daten einfach importiert werden könnten.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eMomentan ist die Seite noch nicht wirklich an Mobilgeräte angepasst, am besten also per PC oder Tablet versuchen.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eBitte kommt mit Feedback und Vorschlägen gerne per Whatsapp oder im Traning auf mich zu.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003e  Cheers, Chris\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n    \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\u003c/div\u003e\r\n{% endblock %} ",
      "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/templates/index.html"
    },
    {
